perturbation.original_text: 3. Disclosure . All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure , except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity; (b) be disclosed only to employees of P a r t y T w o who have executed written confidentiality agreements wit h the employer; and (c) be reproduced or used by P a r t y T w o only to the extent necessary to fulfill it’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Disclosure . All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure , except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity; (b) be disclosed only to employees of P a r t y T w o who have executed written confidentiality agreements wit h the employer; and (c) be reproduced or used by P a r t y T w o only to the extent necessary to fulfill it’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the duration of confidentiality for general disclosures from 'three (3) years' to 'one (1) year'. This directly contradicts Section 15, where 'Term' of the entire agreement is stated to be 'Three (3) Years'. This conflicting durations now create uncertainty about the actual term of confidentiality and agreement's overall term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Term . This agreement shall r emain in effect for a period of Three (3) Years or the life of any Agreements, Contracts or Commercial Arrangements which may arise from the joint efforts of the parties hereto, whichever shall be longer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality period from three years to one year for general disclosures. The contradicted text states the agreement's term is three years. This creates a direct conflict (an inconsistency as per the definition) regarding the duration of the agreement and the confidentiality obligations, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Party One and P a r t y Two shall protect shared Confidential Information with the same degree of care as it normally exercises to protect its own confidential information of similar nature, but at a minimum with a reasonable degree of care to prevent its unauthorized use, dissemination or publication.
perturbation.changed_text: Party One and P a r t y Two shall protect shared Confidential Information, without any obligation to exercise any specific degree of care, to prevent its unauthorized use, dissemination or publication.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the obligation for either party to exercise any specific degree of care in protecting confidential information. This conflicts with Section 10 ('Remedies'), which implies a reasonable standard of care is expected to protect proprietary interests. By eliminating the 'degree of care' clause, it becomes unclear if a party's failure to protect confidential information can be considered a breach if they didn't actively contribute to the disclosure, thus impacting the enforceability of remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies . Party One and P a r t y T w o each acknowledge that compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is necessary to protec t their proprietary interes ts. Each party further acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure to any person or entity in breach of this Agreement will result in irreparable and continuing damage, and that each party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain immediate injun ctive relief and any other rights or remedies to which it may be entitled. If either party violates any of the above agreements, it acknowledges that money damages will be an inadequate remedy and that the violated party will be entitled to specific perfo rmance or to injunctive relief to prohibit the violating party from continuing to violate this agreement even if no money damages can be proven.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation for either party to exercise any specific degree of care in protecting confidential information, while the contradicted text ('Remedies') implies a reasonable standard of care is expected to protect proprietary interests. By eliminating the 'degree of care' clause, it becomes unclear if a party's failure to protect confidential information can be considered a breach if they didn't actively contribute to the disclosure, thus impacting the enforceability of remedies. Therefore, a strong contradiction is created.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information of the other par ty where compelled by law to so disclose pursuant to any legal, judicial or administrative proceeding, provided the disclosing party provides all reasonable prior notice to the other party to allow it to seek a protective order or other appropriate relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information of the other par ty where compelled by law to so disclose pursuant to any legal, judicial or administrative proceeding, without any obligation to provide prior notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. By removing the requirement to provide 'reasonable prior notice' before disclosing confidential information under legal compulsion, this directly contradicts Section 7 ('Disclosure Required by Law'). Section 7 explicitly requires 'reasonable advance notice'. Removing this requirement in Section 3 creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the procedure for legally compelled disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Disclosure Required by Law . Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in paragraphs 3 and 4 hereof, the Confidential Information may be disclo sed to the extent that such disclosure is necessary to comply with any law, regulation, or order of court, provided that each party shall give the other party reasonable advance notice of any such proposed disclosure, shall use its reasonable best efforts to secure an agreement in writing to be bound by the provisions of paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement from any person obtaining access to the Confidential Information pursuant to this paragraph 7, and shall advise the other party in writing of the manner of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation to provide 'reasonable prior notice' before disclosing confidential information under legal compulsion. This directly contradicts Section 7, which explicitly requires 'reasonable advance notice'. Therefore, the modification introduces a clear and direct conflict, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Property Rights, Return of Materials . All materials, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Property Rights, Return of Materials . All materials, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party may, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, retain all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by changing 'shall' to 'may' regarding the return of materials. Initially, both parties were obligated to return all confidential materials upon completion or request. Now, they have the option to retain them. This contradicts the implied obligation to protect and not use the Confidential Information outlined in Section 4 ('Use'), because retention could lead to future unauthorized use. Now the parties can retain the documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Use. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to use the other party's Confidential Information for its own use or for any purpose except for the purpose described in Section 1. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others, without the express written permission of the other party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 to state that each party 'may' retain confidential materials upon completion or request, whereas previously they 'shall' return them. This directly contradicts Section 4, which requires both parties to not use the other party's Confidential Information for its own use or for any purpose except the one outlined in Section 1 and prohibits disclosure. Retaining the confidential information gives them the opportunity to use it, thus creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Term . This agreement shall r emain in effect for a period of Three (3) Years or the life of any Agreements, Contracts or Commercial Arrangements which may arise from the joint efforts of the parties hereto, whichever shall be longer.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Term . This agreement shall r emain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The change in section 15 from a 'Three (3) Years' term to 'indefinitely' directly contradicts Section 9 ('Non-solicitation'). Section 9 explicitly states a period of '18 months following the date of this Agreement' for non-solicitation. If the agreement is now indefinite, it's unclear how the 18-month non-solicitation period applies. The intext contradiction introduces legal uncertainty regarding the duration of both the agreement and specific clauses within it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Non-solicitation: Each party agrees that, for a period of 18 months following the date of this Agree ment, it shall not solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with any of the officers, employees, directors or consultants of the other party which whom such party has had contact or were identified to such party in connection with its evalu ation of a possible transaction between the parties. The term “solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with” shall not be deemed to include generalized searches by Party One for employees or consultants through media advertisements, emplo yment firms or otherwise, that are not focused on persons employed by or who consult for P a r t y T w o .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement term from 'Three (3) Years' to 'indefinitely'. The contradicted text specifies an 18-month non-solicitation period following the agreement date. An indefinite agreement term creates a direct contradiction, making the 18-month non-solicitation period's endpoint unclear and therefore unenforceable. This fits the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties, creating a strong contradiction.
